title: hieracium leucophaeum subsp. godetii
intro: Hieracium leucophaeum subsp. godetii, also known as Godet's Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) native to Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Hieracium leucophaeum subsp. godetii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions when used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
